title: From cheering low gas cost to taking pride 'when oil prices go up': Trump’s evolving stance

This change follows strikes against Iran, which triggered a surge in global oil and petrol prices, impacting the Strait of Hormuz. The administration is grappling with economic consequences and political considerations ahead of midterm elections.

description: Yuzvendra Chahal arrived in Dehradun for Kuldeep Yadav's wedding in Mussoorie, expressing excitement for the festivities. The spinner is set to tie the knot with fiancée Vanshika on March 14 at the Welcomhotel The Savoy Mussoorie. The event is anticipated to be a private yet star-studded affair with several prominent cricketers expected to attend.

description: The Supreme Court declined to mandate menstrual leave for women students and employees nationwide, citing concerns about potential negative impacts on women's employment and the risk of reinforcing gender stereotypes. The court emphasized that policy decisions of this nature should be made by government policymakers after consulting stakeholders, rather than through judicial intervention.

Afghan forces claimed to have targeted Pakistani military centers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, including a fortress and command center, following alleged "night-time violations" along the disputed Durand Line. This escalation intensifies existing tensions between the two nations.

description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor praised Prime Minister Modi and EAM Jaishankar for engaging with Iran amidst escalating West Asian tensions. He urged a collective push for peace, highlighting the risks to Indian citizens in the Gulf and the nation's role as a constructive voice.

This joyous occasion marks the end of Ramadan. Families will reunite for prayers, feasts, and to exchange greetings. Children will receive Eidi gifts.

Palantir CEO Alex Karp admits using Anthropic's Claude after Pentagon ban
description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp confirmed continued use of Anthropic's Claude AI models despite the Pentagon labeling the company a supply chain risk.  The Department of Defense is planning a phase-out, but acknowledges a transition will take time, with potential exemptions for mission-critical operations. This highlights the challenges in disentangling deeply embedded AI systems from defense operations.

title: This tiny village in Maharashtra has a magical secret that grows inside pumpkins
description: Miraj, a historic city in Maharashtra, is renowned for its musical instrument making. Craftsmen create exquisite sitars and tanpuras using a special type of pumpkin. This tradition, passed down through generations, faces threats from climate change impacting pumpkin size. The craft requires government support for survival. The disappearance of this art would mean losing music, history, and ancestral voices.

description: The US trade representative's office has launched Section 301 investigations into 60 economies, including India, for failing to address forced labor in imports. New tariffs on several countries are possible by summer.

title: LPG shortage: What Infosys and HCL Tech's advisory to employees says
description: IT giants Infosys and HCLTech are implementing temporary measures due to an LPG gas shortage impacting India. Infosys has adjusted food court menus and cooking methods across its Bengaluru, Pune, and Chennai campuses. HCLTech has advised its Chennai employees to work from home as cafeteria vendors face operational challenges.

The lawsuit claims the deal was an "unlawful contract" driven by fear for reputations and the CEO's job, not shareholder value.

title: US-Iran war: How Strait of Hormuz closure impacts global oil supply - explained in charts
description: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route, is effectively blocked due to the escalating US-Israeli war on Iran, halting tankers traffic and forcing oil producers to cut the oil output. Damaged infrastructure and blocked shipping lanes are causing global supply shocks, leading to surging energy prices and economic strain worldwide.
